user.title: Connect to SPR
user.description: Enter your SPR router's URL and a read-only API token. Create the token in the SPR UI (Auth → API Tokens) with the scoped path `/plugins/home_assistant/:r`.

data_description.url: The router's address, including scheme and port — its LAN IP or hostname over HTTPS
data_description.token: An SPR token scoped to /plugins/home_assistant/:r — it can only read
data_description.verify_ssl: Disable for SPR's default self-signed certificate

discovery_confirm.title: Set up {name}
discovery_confirm.description: Home Assistant found the SPR router “{name}”. Enter a read-only API token to finish. Create it in the SPR UI (Auth → API Tokens) scoped to `/plugins/home_assistant/:r`.

data_description.url: Confirm the router address before entering your token
data_description.token: An SPR token scoped to /plugins/home_assistant/:r — it can only read
data_description.verify_ssl: Disable for SPR's default self-signed certificate

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate with SPR
reauth_confirm.description: The API token was revoked or rotated on the router. Enter the new read-only token.

abort.already_configured: This SPR router is already configured

data.track_new_devices: Enable tracking for newly discovered devices

data_description.consider_home: How long a device stays Home after it was last seen. Prevents phones that sleep their Wi-Fi from flapping.
data_description.track_new_devices: When off, trackers for new devices are added disabled and can be enabled per device.

wake_on_lan.name: Wake on LAN
wake_on_lan.description: Sends a Wake on LAN magic packet from the SPR router to a device on its network.

mac.name: MAC address
mac.description: MAC address of the device to wake.
